items.summary: This is the last day for deals on Fellow Series 1 espresso machine, Fellow Aiden, and Fellow Stagg kettle. Each is the best in their category.

items.summary: Move over, spring cleaning. These vacuum deals will give your home the cleanest summer yet thanks to Amazon Prime Day.

items.summary: From MacBooks to gaming laptops, these are the very best deals on some of my very favorite laptops for Amazon Prime Day.

items.title: Corgi, the buzzy Y Combinator-backed insurance tech startup, says it didn’t steal an open source product
items.summary: Corgi became embroiled in controversy when Papermark accused it of stealing its software. Corgi says it did not, raising new questions about vibe coding.

items.title: Novak Djokovic has a new job —  advisor to private equity firm General Atlantic
items.summary: General Atlantic has tapped tennis legend Novak Djokovic to serve as a global strategic advisor.

items.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
items.summary: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes kill 920 people as families desperate for news
items.summary: Hundreds are still feared trapped under the rubble, as international rescue teams start to arrive.

items.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
items.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

items.title: US conducts strikes on Iran after attack on cargo ship
items.summary: US Central Command says it has struck missile and drone storage facilities and coastal radar positions.

items.title: CFTC is conducting an investigation into Polymarket, source says
items.summary: The probe comes after the Commodity Futures Trading Commission and the Department of Justice dropped previous inquiries into Polymarket last July.

items.title: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari says he expects a rate hike this year
items.summary: Kashkari said he sees a hike likely this year as the economy continues to feel the hit from spiking inflation.

items.title: OpenAI is reportedly delaying its IPO. Here's when Kalshi traders think it will announce
items.summary: Speculators think the OpenAI IPO will come early next year, with only one-in-three odds it happens in 2026 but a high likelihood it is done by June 2027.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: House Democrat: ‘No question’ Trump will try to interfere with elections
items.summary: Rep. Robert Garcia (D-Calif.) on Thursday re-upped Democratic-led warnings about election interference ahead of the midterm elections. Democratic lawmakers have repeatedly voiced concerns about the Trump administration’s efforts to access voter data from many U.S. states and cited President Trump’s false claims of widespread voter fraud in the 2020 presidential election. “There is no question…

items.title: Buttigieg targeted in false report, police say
items.summary: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said Friday that he was briefly separated from his 4-year-old twin children after an anonymous caller reported false accusations to Child Protective Services (CPS).  In a Substack post titled “A Terrible Thing Happened to My Family,” the former Biden administration official said he was the subject of a “swatting” incident,…

items.title: Trump’s religious liberty report calls for building ‘bridges’ between church and state
items.summary: A draft final report from President Trump’s Religious Liberty Commission released Friday calls for “building bridges between church and state,” a reversal of a long-standing U.S. legal principle.  “Americans must know their rights and stand with courage when those rights are challenged,” the commission’s report read. “To preserve this freedom, we must build bridges, not walls,…

items.title: Einstein Probe may have caught a black hole tearing apart a white dwarf for the first time
items.summary: Astronomers may have witnessed one of the rarest and most dramatic cosmic events ever seen: a long-sought intermediate-mass black hole ripping apart a dense white dwarf star and devouring it. The Einstein Probe space telescope caught the explosion in its earliest moments, revealing an unusual sequence of intense X-ray flashes unlike anything seen in a typical gamma-ray burst.

items.title: They knew the pill was fake but their memory still improved
items.summary: Healthy older adults experienced measurable improvements in memory, physical performance, and stress after taking placebo pills for just three weeks. The most surprising finding was that the placebo often worked even when participants knew the pills were completely inactive.

items.title: After 70 years of excavation, ancient Sardis becomes a UNESCO World Heritage site
items.summary: After nearly seven decades of excavation, the legendary ancient city of Sardis has become a UNESCO World Heritage Site, celebrating years of discoveries that continue to reshape its history. Archaeologists say the biggest breakthroughs don't happen in a single season—they emerge as decades of evidence slowly come together.

items.title: How big a cybersecurity threat are the latest AI models, really?
items.summary: New AI models are accelerating the game of cat-and-mouse as cybersecurity experts try to keep ahead of would-be hackers. An AI expert explains the risks.

items.title: Giant, deep-sea roly-polies steal a gene to endure starvation
items.summary: The enormous deep-sea cousins of your garden’s pill bugs can go five years without food. A gene they pilfered from bacteria may be part of the secret.

items.title: Brains break and repair DNA to grow
items.summary: Newborn mice neurons can snap both DNA strands to migrate, then repair the breaks within a day. The process may be a normal part of brain development.
